wpcs_compliance.analysis: Mostly follows WPCS: snake_case method name, correct add_action() syntax. load_plugin_textdomain uses basename(dirname(__FILE__)) which is WPCS-acceptable. Mixed indentation (spaces vs tabs) is a minor deviation.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL queries. Constructor registers hooks and loads textdomain only. Zero database surface.

security.analysis: Registers admin_notices hook which fires a dependency check. No direct user input consumed in this function.

performance.analysis: Three operations: parent constructor, textdomain load, and one add_action(). O(1) cost, runs once at plugin load.

wp_api_usage.analysis: load_plugin_textdomain() called with correct parameters. parent::__construct() uses standard WP_Widget signature. add_action() hooks admin_notices correctly.

code_quality.analysis: Single-responsibility constructor. Inline textdomain load inside constructor is slightly non-standard but functional. Well-named class methods.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on WP_Widget parent class and JOB_MANAGER_VERSION from WP Job Manager. The admin_notices hook to wpjm_check_installed() gracefully handles missing dependency.

i18n.analysis: Uses __() with 'wpjmfilter' text domain. load_plugin_textdomain() loads translations from /languages. Both strings in constructor are correctly wrapped.

accessibility.analysis: Widget constructor defers HTML rendering to separate display methods. No accessibility concerns at this initialization layer.

reasoning.key_observation: Correctly structured WP_Widget constructor with load_plugin_textdomain() and dependency-check via admin_notices hook.

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Proper WPCS: tab indentation, snake_case variables, foreach loop style. sanitize_text_field callback in register_setting() is correct.

sql_safety.analysis: get_option() and update_option() safely. No raw SQL. Migration loop uses WordPress option API.

security.analysis: current_user_can('unfiltered_html') check restricts meta box to authorized users only. register_setting() uses sanitize_text_field for sanitization callbacks.

performance.analysis: Migration loop calls get_option() twice per option — could batch. get_post_types() iteration for meta box registration is O(n post types). Acceptable for admin_init.

wp_api_usage.analysis: register_setting() with sanitization callbacks, current_user_can(), add_meta_box() with esc_html__(), add_action('save_post'). All correct.

code_quality.analysis: Migration code accumulates over versions. Dual get_option() calls per option could be optimized. Standard WP plugin migration pattern.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Depends on core register_setting(), add_meta_box(). Internal $this->meta_setup and post_meta_save referenced.

i18n.analysis: esc_html__() for meta box title with correct text domain.

accessibility.analysis: 'unfiltered_html' capability guard prevents untrusted users from adding arbitrary scripts via meta box.

reasoning.key_observation: Secure admin_init with current_user_can('unfiltered_html') restriction on meta box and sanitize_text_field callbacks.

wpcs_compliance.analysis: Proper class_exists() and defined() guards. Correct define() usage. Brace alignment and indentation follow WPCS. trailingslashit() used correctly.

sql_safety.analysis: No SQL. Class loader and file inclusion.

security.analysis: Guards prevent duplicate definition and class loading. require_once() with constants prevents path traversal. Constants are fixed at plugin load time.

performance.analysis: Multiple require_once() at plugin init. spl_autoload_register() is efficient for lazy class loading.

wp_api_usage.analysis: trailingslashit() and dirname() used correctly. l10ni18n() call ensures translations before class load.

code_quality.analysis: Excellent defensive programming: class_exists() guard, double-define protection for both constants. l10ni18n() before class load ensures correct i18n order.

dependency_integrity.analysis: Manages own dependencies via spl_autoload_register. cmb2_bootstrap() abstracts final init.

i18n.analysis: l10ni18n() called before class loading — correct i18n initialization order.

accessibility.analysis: No HTML output. Library bootstrap.

reasoning.key_observation: Well-defended library bootstrap with double-guard against duplicate class/constant definitions and correct l10ni18n() load order.
